title: Add regulation-aware dry/wet and session-type specialists
summary: FIA rules create hard strategic asymmetries: dry races require two dry compounds, while wet/intermediate usage changes that requirement; Sprints have no mandatory stop requirement.
rationale: FIA rules create hard strategic asymmetries: dry races require two dry compounds, while wet/intermediate usage changes that requirement; Sprints have no mandatory stop requirement. Those rules imply different base rates and different meanings of stint/compound features.
implementation_hint: Create specialist models or gating features for dry-only rows versus wet/intermediate rows, and for likely grand-prix-style sessions versus atypical sessions such as testing. Add legality features like dry-compound-switch-needed, already-used-wet-tyre proxy, and stint-stage relative to mandatory-stop satisfaction.
expected_effect: Should reduce regime mixing and make probabilities more coherent in late-race dry stints, wet races, and atypical sessions.
risk: Session-type inference may be imperfect if metadata is incomplete, and hard rules can mislead when races are suspended or strategy is heavily disrupted.
